picks.rationale: The Milwaukee Brewers hold a significant pitching advantage with Jacob Misiorowski, an NL Cy Young favorite boasting a 1.47 ERA, taking the mound against a likely opener or less established pitcher for the Cardinals. The Brewers' offense is also in strong form, with a 42.2% hard-hit rate and a 105 wRC+ over their last seven games. Milwaukee has dominated the season series with a 5-1 record against St. Louis. The line movement, with the Brewers' moneyline shifting from -170 to as high as -210 in some markets, indicates sharp money backing Milwaukee.

The Chicago Cubs present a strong value pick due to the pitching matchup and historical head-to-head performance. Cubs starter Matthew Boyd has shown improved recent form, allowing only five runs in his last 15.2 innings and previously throwing seven shutout innings against the Orioles. Conversely, Orioles starter Shane Baz struggled in his last outing against the Cubs, giving up five runs in just 2.1 innings. The Cubs also boast a dominant 5-1 record against the Orioles in their last six matchups over the past three seasons.

Despite Paul Skenes starting for the Pirates, he is currently on a six-start losing streak and has given up 11 runs in his last two outings, suggesting a vulnerability. Both the Braves and Pirates offenses have been performing well against right-handed pitching recently, ranking in the top-8 over the last 10 days. The Pirates have a strong home trend towards the Over, cashing in 30 of their last 45 home games. Furthermore, the total line has moved from an opening of 7.5 or 8 runs to 9, with the Over still attracting action, indicating confidence in a high-scoring affair.

picks.game: New York Yankees @ Tampa Bay Rays
picks.rationale: The Tampa Bay Rays are well-positioned for a bounce-back win at home against a struggling Yankees lineup. The Yankees are severely hampered by injuries to key power hitters Aaron Judge and Giancarlo Stanton, both on the 10-day IL, significantly impacting their offense. Rays starter Ian Seymour's swing-and-miss profile is a strong fit against a Yankees lineup that has been prone to whiffs. Conversely, Yankees starter Will Warren struggles against low-whiff lineups, which the Rays are, attacking a key weakness. The Rays, despite a recent three-game skid, are motivated to turn things around at Tropicana Field.

The Phillies are coming off an embarrassing 15-1 loss where their ace, Cristopher Sanchez, allowed nine earned runs, which could impact team morale and offensive rhythm. Philadelphia's lineup has historically struggled against left-handed pitching, batting a mere .223 this season. Reds starter Andrew Abbott has been effective at limiting hits, allowing under 5.5 hits in 10 of his last 12 starts, including a strong outing against the Phillies in May. With Zack Wheeler, who boasts a 2.36 ERA, on the mound for the Phillies, a lower-scoring game is anticipated despite the hitter-friendly Great American Ball Park.

The total goals market has seen significant sharp money backing the Under, pushing the line from -138 to -160, indicating strong market confidence in a low-scoring affair. Both Colombia and Switzerland have demonstrated formidable defenses in the current World Cup, with Colombia conceding only one goal and Switzerland just three across their four matches each. Furthermore, both teams are grappling with crucial attacking injuries; Colombia's starting striker Jhon Córdoba is out for the tournament, and Switzerland is missing key playmaker Johan Manzambi, along with Djibril Sow and Ruben Vargas, severely limiting their offensive firepower. This combination of defensive strength, key absences, and line movement strongly supports a tight, low-scoring contest.

Colombia is considered the clear betting favorite and is favored to advance to the quarterfinals, reflecting their strong position in this matchup. Their head-to-head record against Switzerland is also favorable, including a 2-0 victory in their only previous World Cup encounter and a 3-1 win in their most recent meeting. While Colombia is without striker Jhon Córdoba, their defense has been exceptional in the World Cup, securing three clean sheets and conceding just one goal in four games. Conversely, Switzerland faces significant challenges with key attacking players Johan Manzambi, Djibril Sow, and Ruben Vargas all confirmed out due to injury, which will severely hinder their ability to penetrate Colombia's solid backline.
